hooks.prompt: Review the Supabase code change for issues. Check for: 1) RLS (Row Level Security) not enabled on tables with user data (any authenticated user can read/write all rows — enable RLS and create policies: ALTER TABLE t ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY), 2) Using service_role key in client-side code (bypasses RLS — client must use anon key only; service_role is server-side only), 3) Supabase client instantiated per request instead of shared singleton (connection overhead — create one client instance and reuse, especially with createClient in server components), 4) Missing foreign key constraints or database-level validation (data integrity depends on application logic only — enforce constraints in PostgreSQL schema), 5) realtime subscription without cleanup on unmount (memory leak and stale subscriptions — call channel.unsubscribe() in useEffect cleanup), 6) Storage bucket without RLS policies (public access to all files — create bucket policies restricting access by auth.uid() or file path patterns), 7) Using .select('*') on all queries (over-fetching — select only needed columns for performance, especially with joins), 8) Missing error handling on Supabase client calls (errors return { data: null, error } not exceptions — always check error field before using data), 9) Edge Function without proper CORS headers for browser access (blocked by browser — add Access-Control-Allow-Origin and handle OPTIONS preflight), 10) Database migration that modifies RLS policies without testing (broken policies = data exposure or lockout — test policies with different user roles before deploying). Return {"ok": true} if no issues, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue found"} if problems detected.
